input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for their project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to first understand the project structure, then set up and run tests with coverage, and finally analyze the results to identify risk hotspots.

Let me start by exploring the project structure to understand what we're working with.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-c54ad7066fd7417892c32369e8a15878 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -30
arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with code coverage collection

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. It's a .NET 9.0 solution with:
- A main project with `StudentService.cs` containing 4 methods: `Enroll`, `CalculateGpa`, `FindById`, `Search`
- A test project using MSTest with `coverlet.collector` already referenced
- Tests cover `Enroll` (partially), `FindById`, and `Search` (partially)
- `CalculateGpa` has NO tests at all (the comment confirms this is intentional)

Let me run the tests with coverage collection and then analyze the results.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-c54ad7066fd7417892c32369e8a15878 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -30
arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection
